---
title: "LRPITT12: Joint ITT analysis across the suite outcomes"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_convergence.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRPITT12 estimates the joint posterior over the treatment effects of the LRPITT
suite, using the RCT phase only. It delivers cross-outcome contrasts (e.g.
"is $\tau_L$ more positive than $\tau_W$?") and acts as a consistency check on the
single-outcome LRPITT models. The proximal $\tau_k$ should track their
single-outcome counterparts closely; the distal outcomes (R, E, UR, UE) carry the
tiered $\mathrm{Normal}(0, 0.3)$ prior in the single-outcome suite but the untiered
$\mathrm{Normal}(0, 0.5)$ prior here, so they may differ by a **prior-driven** amount
that is not Monte Carlo noise (and the joint is fit on $n = 53$ vs the
single-outcome $n = 54$).

The model is a **linear Beta-Binomial across ten outcomes** with shared predictor
structure mirroring the single-outcome suite: outcome-specific intercepts,
treatment effects, own-baseline + linear-age **precision** terms (no
cross-baselines — the ITT effect is identified by the empty adjustment set), and
outcome-specific dispersion; no age GP and no LKJ residual correlation.

::: {.callout-note title="Outcome set: ten of the eleven suite measures"}

The joint covers the **baseline-bearing** outcomes
$(\tau_{TR}, \tau_{TE}, \tau_{UR}, \tau_{UE}, \tau_R, \tau_E, \tau_L, \tau_B, \tau_P, \tau_W)$.

- **Nonword (N) is excluded**: it is post-only and its degenerate baseline cannot
  enter the joint's dense pre-score matrix. Its treatment effect is read from the
  single-outcome **off-floor** model LRPITT11.
- **Phonetic spelling (P) is included as a graded outcome** (its floored baseline
  simply shrinks $\gamma^{\text{own}}_P$); the binary off-floor primary for P is in
  LRPITT09. The joint $\tau_P$ here is the graded effect — read it alongside the
  off-floor result, not on its own.

:::

## Structure

For child $i$ and outcome $k$:

$$
\eta_{k,i} = \alpha_k + \tau_k G_i
           + \gamma^{\text{own}}_k\, \text{logit}_*(k_{\text{pre},i}, N_k)
           + \gamma^{A}_k\, A_{\text{std},i},
$$

with a per-outcome Beta-Binomial likelihood, $\mu_{k,i} = \sigma(\eta_{k,i})$, and
outcome-specific dispersion $\kappa_k$. Observations with a missing post-score are
masked per-outcome. **Sign convention:** $G = 1$ is the intervention arm, so a
**positive** $\tau_k$ means the intervention raised outcome $k$.

The LKJ residual-correlation and age-GP flags are off by default (both
prior-dominated / divergence-inducing at this scale; re-enable via
`ModelSpec.extra` for a sensitivity fit).

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_diagnostics.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_results_joint.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
